name: Sayf ibn al-Harith
title: The Loving Youth
role: Brave Companion and Ally of Malik
shortDescription: A brave youth from the Bani Jabir tribe who, along with his cousin Malik, went to the battlefield with tears of joy for martyrdom.
fullStory:
Sayf ibn al-Harith (or Harith ibn Sari') was one of the valiant youths and pure Shias of Basra (or Kufa). He and his cousin 'Malik ibn Abd', who were also maternal brothers, were inseparable allies. These two young men reached the Imam's companions in Karbala before the Hussaini caravan was completely surrounded.

On the day of Ashura, after the martyrdom of Hanzala ibn As'ad, Sayf and Malik came together to Imam Hussain (AS). They stood before the Imam weeping. Imam Hussain (AS), seeing their tears, asked: 'O cousins! Why are you crying? By God, I hope your eyes will soon be brightened'.

Sayf and Malik replied: 'May God sacrifice us for you! By God, we are not crying for ourselves; our tears are because we see you surrounded by the enemy, and we cannot sacrifice anything more than our lives to defend you'. This statement showcased the peak of their love and devotion.

Hearing this loving statement, Imam Hussain (AS) said: 'May God grant you the reward of the pious'. Then Sayf and Malik entered the battlefield side by side. They looked at each other in the field to surpass one another in combat.

These two brave youths fought with unparalleled coordination; whenever one was surrounded, the other rushed to his aid. Finally, after an epic battle and killing dozens of enemies, both were martyred next to each other, continuing their friendship in Paradise.
quoteArabic: نَبْكي عَلَيْكَ نَراكَ قَدْ أُحيطَ بِكَ وَلا نَقْدِرُ أَنْ نَمْنَعَكَ
quote: We cry for you, seeing you surrounded, unable to sacrifice more than our lives for you.
